meta.title: Guess Papers (High Chance)

subcategories.title: Food Inspector Sindh Guess Paper - 100 MCQs

questions.q: Choose the correct synonym for 'Exempt':
questions.ex: Exempt means free from an obligation or requirement.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Contaminated':
questions.ex: Pure is the opposite of contaminated (polluted/impure).

questions.q: He is very good ___ Mathematics.
questions.ex: The correct preposition is 'good at'.

questions.q: Choose the correct spelling:
questions.ex: The correct spelling is 'Adulteration'.

questions.q: A person who does not eat meat is called a:
questions.ex: Vegetarians do not eat meat.

questions.q: Meaning of 'To smell a rat':
questions.ex: It means to suspect something is wrong or a trick is being played.

questions.q: Identify the Part of Speech: 'The food was delicious.'
questions.ex: 'Delicious' describes the noun 'food', so it is an adjective.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence:
questions.ex: 'Doesn't like' is the correct singular present negative form.

questions.q: The plural of 'Loaf' is:
questions.ex: Nouns ending in 'f' usually change to 'ves' in plural.

questions.q: Synonym of 'Nutritious':
questions.ex: Nutritious means providing nourishment and being healthy.

questions.q: He has been living here ___ 2010.
questions.ex: 'Since' is used for a specific point in time.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Abundant':
questions.ex: Scarce means in short supply, the opposite of abundant.

questions.q: One who collects and studies stamps:
questions.ex: A philatelist collects stamps.

questions.q: The word 'Fragile' means:
questions.ex: Fragile items break easily.

questions.q: Choose the correct article: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.ex: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound (o), so 'an' is used.

questions.q: Meaning of 'Break the ice':
questions.ex: It means to overcome initial shyness or start a conversation.

questions.q: Synonym of 'Preserve':
questions.ex: To preserve means to keep or maintain in its original state.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Stale' (food):
questions.ex: Fresh is the opposite of stale.

questions.q: Identify the Verb: 'They eat breakfast early.'
questions.ex: 'Eat' is the action word (verb).

questions.q: Choose the correct spelling:
questions.ex: The correct spelling is 'Hygiene'.

questions.q: Which vitamin is essential for blood clotting?
questions.ex: Vitamin K is vital for blood coagulation.

questions.q: Milk is a poor source of which mineral?
questions.ex: Milk contains very little iron.

questions.q: The main component of Honey is:
questions.ex: Fructose is the primary sugar in honey.

questions.q: Which acid is present in Vinegar?
questions.ex: Vinegar contains acetic acid.

questions.q: What is the pH value of pure water?
questions.ex: Pure water is neutral with a pH of 7.

questions.q: Scurvy is caused by the deficiency of:
questions.ex: Vitamin C deficiency causes scurvy.

questions.q: Which gas is used for artificial ripening of fruits?
questions.ex: Ethylene gas is used to ripen fruits artificially.

questions.q: Pasteurization of milk is done at:
questions.ex: Both low-temperature long-time and high-temperature short-time methods are used.

questions.q: Which is the richest source of Vitamin C?
questions.ex: Amla is extremely rich in Vitamin C.

questions.q: Iodine deficiency leads to:
questions.ex: Goitre is the enlargement of the thyroid gland due to lack of iodine.

questions.q: The process of adding nutrients to food is called:
questions.ex: Food fortification increases nutritional value.

questions.q: Which vitamin is also known as Ascorbic Acid?
questions.ex: Ascorbic acid is the chemical name for Vitamin C.

questions.q: Which is the main protein found in Milk?
questions.ex: Casein makes up about 80% of milk protein.

questions.q: What is the full form of FSSAI (relevant to food standards)?
options: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India
questions.ex: Though Indian, it's a standard reference for regional tests.

questions.q: The 'Green Revolution' is associated with:
questions.ex: It focused on increasing agricultural production.

questions.q: Which organ in the human body produces Bile?
questions.ex: The liver produces bile, which is stored in the gallbladder.

questions.q: What is the common name for Sodium Chloride?
questions.ex: NaCl is common table salt.

questions.q: Which vitamin helps in the absorption of Calcium?
questions.ex: Vitamin D is necessary for calcium absorption in the gut.

questions.q: The unit of energy in food is:
questions.ex: Food energy is measured in Calories (kilocalories).

questions.q: Night blindness is caused by deficiency of:
questions.ex: Vitamin A is essential for vision.

questions.q: Which bacteria is responsible for turning milk into curd?
questions.ex: Lactobacillus ferments lactose into lactic acid.

questions.q: The outer layer of the Earth is called:
questions.ex: The crust is the outermost solid shell.

questions.q: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.ex: Jupiter is the largest planet.

questions.q: What is the percentage of Nitrogen in the air?
questions.ex: Air is mostly Nitrogen (78%).

questions.q: Which part of the plant is the Ginger we eat?
questions.ex: Ginger is a modified underground stem (rhizome).

questions.q: What is the chemical symbol for Iron?
questions.ex: Fe comes from the Latin word Ferrum.

questions.q: The study of insects is called:
questions.ex: Entomology is the scientific study of insects.

questions.q: Which is the smallest bird in the world?
questions.ex: The Bee Hummingbird is the smallest.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in Fahrenheit?
questions.ex: Water boils at 212°F (100°C).

questions.q: Which gas is known as Laughing Gas?
questions.ex: Nitrous oxide (N2O) is laughing gas.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Liaquat Ali Khan was the first PM.

questions.q: The largest lake in Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Manchar Lake in Sindh is the largest freshwater lake.

questions.q: Which city of Sindh is famous for its 'Ajrak'?
questions.ex: Bhit Shah and Hala are famous for Ajrak and Sufi culture.

questions.q: The shrine of Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai is located in:
questions.ex: It is in Bhit Shah, Sindh.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Founder of Pakistan'?
questions.ex: Muhammad Ali Jinnah is the Quaid-e-Azam.

questions.q: The first capital of Pakistan was:
questions.ex: Karachi was the capital until 1959.

questions.q: Mohenjo-daro is located in which district?
questions.ex: Mohenjo-daro is in Larkana, Sindh.

questions.q: Which is the national flower of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Jasmine (Chambeli) is the national flower.

questions.q: The highest peak of Sindh is:
questions.ex: Zardak Peak in the Kirthar Mountains is the highest in Sindh.

questions.q: Who wrote the national anthem of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Hafeez Jalandhari wrote the lyrics.

questions.q: Which river is called the 'Life line of Pakistan'?
questions.ex: The Indus River is the longest and most important.

questions.q: The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan was passed during the era of:
questions.ex: Z.A. Bhutto passed the 1973 Constitution.

questions.q: Which is the national game of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Field Hockey is the national game.

questions.q: The 'Kotri Barrage' is built on which river?
questions.ex: Kotri Barrage is on the Indus River near Hyderabad.

questions.q: Who was the first woman Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Benazir Bhutto was the first female PM of a Muslim nation.

questions.q: Which city is known as the 'City of Saints'?
questions.ex: Multan is famous for its many Sufi shrines.

questions.q: The 'Thar Desert' is located in which province?
questions.ex: The Thar Desert is primarily in Sindh.

questions.q: Which is the largest seaport of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Karachi Port is the oldest and largest.

questions.q: How many provinces are there in Pakistan?
questions.ex: There are 4 main provinces (Punjab, Sindh, KPK, Balochistan).

questions.q: The 'Mazar-e-Quaid' is located in:
questions.ex: Jinnah's mausoleum is in Karachi.

questions.q: Which month is the Independence month of Pakistan?
questions.ex: August 14th is Independence Day.

questions.q: The first census in Pakistan was held in:
questions.ex: The first official census was in 1951.

questions.q: Which is the smallest province of Pakistan by area?
questions.ex: KPK is the smallest among the four provinces.

questions.q: The 'Makli Necropolis' is in which city?
questions.ex: Makli is near Thatta, Sindh.

questions.q: Who is the current Governor of Sindh?
questions.ex: Kamran Tessori (as of early 2024/2026 check).

questions.q: How many Surahs are in the Holy Quran?
questions.ex: There are 114 Surahs.

questions.q: Who was the first Caliph of Islam?
questions.ex: Hazrat Abu Bakr RA was the first Caliph.

questions.q: The first migration of Muslims was to:
questions.ex: The first Hijrat was to Abyssinia.

questions.q: In which month was the Quran first revealed?
questions.ex: It was revealed in the month of Ramadan.

questions.q: How many times is Namaz mentioned in the Quran?
questions.ex: Prayer (Salat) is mentioned approximately 700 times.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Zakat'?
questions.ex: Zakat means to purify wealth.

questions.q: Who is called 'Saifullah'?
questions.ex: Khalid bin Walid was titled 'Sword of Allah'.

questions.q: The Battle of Badr was fought in which year?
questions.ex: It was fought in 2 AH.

questions.q: How many pillars of Islam are there?
questions.ex: There are 5 pillars (Shahada, Salat, Zakat, Sawm, Hajj).

questions.q: The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) belonged to which tribe?
questions.ex: He was from the Quraish tribe (Banu Hashim).

questions.q: Which is the longest Surah of the Quran?
questions.ex: Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest.

questions.q: How many Sajdas are in the Holy Quran?
questions.ex: There are 14 obligatory Sajdas.

questions.q: Who was the first woman to embrace Islam?
questions.ex: Hazrat Khadija RA was the first to accept Islam.

questions.q: The Cave of Hira is in which mountain?
questions.ex: The cave is in Jabal-e-Noor.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Islam'?
questions.ex: Islam means peace and submission to Allah.

questions.q: If 5 apples cost 100 rupees, what is the cost of 12 apples?
What is 15% of 200?
Complete the series: 2, 4, 8, 16, ___
questions.ex: The number doubles each time.

questions.q: If 'WATER' is coded as 'XBUFS', then 'BREAD' is?
questions.ex: Each letter is shifted by one position (B->C, R->S, E->F, A->B, D->E).

questions.q: Which is the odd one out?
questions.ex: Juice is from fruit, others are dairy products.

questions.q: What is the square of 13?
How many minutes are in 1.5 hours?
questions.ex: 1.5 * 60 = 90 minutes.

questions.q: A triangle with all three sides equal is called:
questions.ex: Equilateral triangles have equal sides.

questions.q: If today is Monday, what day will it be after 61 days?
questions.ex: 61 / 7 = 8 weeks and 5 days. Monday + 5 days = Saturday.

questions.q: Find the average of 10, 20, 30, 40, 50:
Pakistan left CENTO in?
questions.ex: Pakistan withdrew from CENTO in 1979.

questions.q: When was the Objective Resolution passed?
questions.ex: The Objective Resolution was passed in 1949.

questions.q: When was the first constitution of Pakistan enforced?
questions.ex: Pakistan's first constitution was enforced in 1956.

questions.q: What is the capital of Turkey?
questions.ex: The capital of Turkey is Ankara.

questions.q: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.ex: The Pacific Ocean is the largest.

questions.q: Which blood group is universal donor?
questions.ex: O negative is universal donor.

questions.q: Normal human body temperature?
questions.ex: Normal body temperature is 37°C.

questions.q: Chemical formula of water?
questions.ex: Water is H2O.

questions.q: Sun rises in the?
questions.ex: Sun rises in the east.

questions.q: Light year measures?
questions.ex: Light year measures distance.

questions.q: How many pillars of Islam?
questions.ex: There are five pillars.

questions.q: Hijrah occurred in?
questions.ex: Hijrah occurred in 622 AD.

questions.q: Total Siparas in Quran?
questions.ex: There are 30 Siparas.

questions.q: First revealed Surah?
questions.ex: First revelation was Surah Al-Alaq.

questions.q: Zakat made obligatory in?
questions.ex: Zakat became obligatory in 2 Hijri.

questions.q: Square of 12?
Half of 200?
questions.ex: Half is 100.

questions.q: CPU stands for?
questions.ex: CPU = Central Processing Unit.

questions.q: RAM is?
questions.ex: RAM is volatile.

questions.q: MS Word is?
questions.ex: MS Word is application software.

questions.q: Father of Internet?
questions.ex: Vint Cerf is a pioneer.

questions.q: WWW stands for?
questions.ex: WWW = World Wide Web.

questions.q: Synonym of Brave?
questions.ex: Brave means courageous.

questions.q: Antonym of Fast?
questions.ex: Opposite is slow.

questions.q: Plural of Child?
questions.ex: Correct plural is children.

questions.q: He ___ going to school.
questions.ex: Correct is 'is'.

questions.q: Opposite of Hot?
questions.ex: Hot opposite is cold.

questions.q: Largest continent?
questions.ex: Asia is largest.

questions.q: Smallest continent?
questions.ex: Australia is smallest.

questions.q: Currency of Japan?
questions.ex: Japan uses Yen.

questions.q: UN founded in?
questions.ex: UN founded in 1945.

questions.q: First Governor General of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Quaid-e-Azam was first GG.

questions.q: National animal of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Markhor is national animal.

questions.q: National flower?
questions.ex: Jasmine is national flower.

questions.q: National language?
questions.ex: Urdu is national language.

questions.q: K2 is located in?
questions.ex: K2 is in Pakistan.

questions.q: Indus River originates from?
questions.ex: Originates from Tibet.

questions.q: Largest desert?
questions.ex: Sahara is largest hot desert.

questions.q: Gas used in balloons?
questions.ex: Helium is used.

questions.q: Speed of light?
questions.ex: Speed is 3×10^8 m/s.

questions.q: Unit of force?
questions.ex: Force unit is Newton.

questions.q: Unit of power?
questions.ex: Power unit is Watt.

questions.q: Which planet is red?
questions.ex: Mars is red planet.

questions.q: Closest planet to sun?
questions.ex: Mercury is closest.

questions.q: Largest planet?
questions.ex: Jupiter is largest.

questions.q: Moon is satellite of?
questions.ex: Moon orbits Earth.

questions.q: Earth rotates in?
questions.ex: Earth rotates in 24 hours.

questions.q: Who discovered gravity?
questions.ex: Newton discovered gravity.

questions.q: Boiling point of water?
questions.ex: Water boils at 100°C.

questions.q: Freezing point of water?
questions.ex: Freezing point is 0°C.

questions.q: Largest organ in human body?
questions.ex: Skin is largest organ.

questions.q: Human heart chambers?
questions.ex: Heart has 4 chambers.

questions.q: Synonym of Happy?
questions.ex: Happy means joyful.

questions.q: Antonym of Big?
questions.ex: Opposite is small.

questions.q: Correct spelling?
questions.ex: Correct is receive.

questions.q: He ___ playing.
questions.ex: Correct is is.

questions.q: They ___ going.
questions.ex: Correct is are.

questions.q: Largest country?
questions.ex: Russia is largest.

questions.q: Pakistan independence year?
questions.ex: Pakistan got independence in 1947.

questions.q: Allama Iqbal birth year?
questions.ex: Iqbal born in 1877.

questions.q: Liaquat Ali Khan assassinated in?
questions.ex: He was assassinated in 1951.

questions.q: Minar-e-Pakistan located in?

questions.q: Square root of 81?
Keyboard is?

questions.q: Monitor is?
questions.ex: Monitor is output device.

questions.q: Hard disk is?
questions.ex: Hard disk stores data.

questions.q: 1 byte = ? bits
questions.ex: 1 byte = 8 bits.

questions.q: Binary digits are?
questions.ex: Binary uses 0 and 1.

questions.q: Pakistan PM is?
questions.ex: Head of govt is PM.

subcategories.title: FIA Assistant Sub Inspector (ASI) Guess Paper - 100 MCQs

questions.q: Choose the synonym of 'Abstain':
questions.ex: Abstain means to refrain from doing something.

questions.q: Choose the antonym of 'Fragile':
questions.ex: Fragile means easily broken; robust means strong and healthy.

questions.q: He is '___' unique person.
questions.ex: 'Unique' starts with a consonant 'y' sound, so 'a' is used.

questions.q: Identify the correct spelling:
questions.ex: The correct spelling is 'Accommodation' (double c, double m).

questions.q: Change to Passive Voice: 'He opens the door.'
options: The door is opened by him.
The door was opened by him.
The door is open by him.
The door has opened by him.
questions.ex: Simple present 'opens' becomes 'is opened' in passive voice.

questions.q: Choose the correct preposition: 'He is fond ___ music.'
questions.ex: 'Fond' is always followed by the preposition 'of'.

questions.q: A person who hates mankind is called:
questions.ex: A misanthrope is someone who dislikes or avoids human society.

questions.q: Meaning of the idiom 'To cry wolf':
questions.ex: To cry wolf means to raise a false alarm.

questions.q: Identify the part of speech: 'The cat sat SILENTLY.'
questions.ex: 'Silently' describes how the cat sat, making it an adverb.

questions.q: Complete the sentence: 'If it rains, I ___ stay home.'
questions.ex: This is a first conditional sentence (If + present, will + base verb).

questions.q: Synonym of 'Enormous':
questions.ex: Enormous means very large or huge.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Vague':
questions.ex: Vague means unclear; clear is its opposite.

questions.q: She is '___' best student in class.
questions.ex: Superlative degrees ('best') take the definite article 'the'.

questions.q: One who can neither read nor write is:
questions.ex: Illiterate is the term for someone who cannot read or write.

questions.q: Meaning of 'Break the ice':
questions.ex: 'Break the ice' means to initiate social interaction.

questions.q: He is afraid '___' dogs.
questions.ex: 'Afraid' is followed by the preposition 'of'.

questions.q: Synonym of 'Precise':
questions.ex: Precise means exact or accurate.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Ancient':
questions.ex: Ancient means very old; modern is the opposite.

questions.q: I '___' working since morning.
questions.ex: Present perfect continuous ('have been') is used for actions starting in the past and continuing.

questions.q: Plural of 'Mouse':
questions.ex: The irregular plural of mouse is mice.

questions.q: The headquarter of Interpol is in:
questions.ex: Interpol is headquartered in Lyon, France.

questions.q: Which is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.ex: Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world.

questions.q: The currency of Turkey is:
questions.ex: The Turkish Lira is the official currency of Turkey.

questions.q: Which planet is known as the 'Blue Planet'?
questions.ex: Earth is called the Blue Planet due to its abundant water.

questions.q: Who is the current Secretary-General of the UN?
questions.ex: António Guterres is the current UN Secretary-General.

questions.q: The 'Great Wall' is located in which country?
questions.ex: The Great Wall of China is a famous landmark in China.

questions.q: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.ex: The Nile is generally considered the longest river in the world.

questions.q: The Nobel Prize is awarded in how many categories?
questions.ex: Nobel Prizes are awarded in Physics, Chemistry, Physiology/Medicine, Literature, Peace, and Economics (6 categories).

questions.q: The largest producer of oil in the world is:
questions.ex: As of recent years, the USA has become the top oil producer.

questions.q: Mount Everest is located in which country?
questions.ex: Mount Everest is located in the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and China, with the summit in Nepal.

questions.q: The deepest ocean in the world is:
questions.ex: The Pacific Ocean is the deepest and largest ocean.

questions.q: The 'Statue of Liberty' was a gift from which country to the USA?
questions.ex: France gifted the Statue of Liberty to the USA.

questions.q: The capital of Russia is:
questions.ex: Moscow is the capital of Russia.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Geometry'?
questions.ex: Euclid is often referred to as the Father of Geometry.

questions.q: The largest continent by population is:
questions.ex: Asia is the most populous continent.

questions.q: The 'Red Cross' was founded by:
questions.ex: Henry Dunant founded the International Committee of the Red Cross.

questions.q: Which country has the largest parliament in the world?
questions.ex: The National People's Congress of China is the largest parliamentary body.

questions.q: The capital of Australia is:
questions.ex: Canberra is the capital city of Australia.

questions.q: The 'Eiffel Tower' is in:
questions.ex: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France.

questions.q: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.ex: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key to 'Undo' in Windows?
questions.ex: Ctrl+Z is the shortcut for Undo.

questions.q: Which of the following is an Operating System?
questions.ex: Linux is a famous open-source operating system.

questions.q: 1 Gigabyte (GB) is equal to:
questions.ex: 1 GB = 1024 Megabytes.

questions.q: The 'Brain' of the computer is:
questions.ex: The CPU performs all processing, hence called the brain.

questions.q: What does 'HTTP' stand for?
questions.ex: HTTP is the foundation of data communication for the WWW.

questions.q: Which key is used to refresh a webpage?
questions.ex: F5 is the standard key to refresh or reload a page.

questions.q: Which of the following is 'Permanent' storage?
questions.ex: ROM (Read-Only Memory) is non-volatile and permanent.

questions.q: What is the extension of an MS Word file (modern)?
questions.ex: .docx is the default extension for MS Word documents.

questions.q: A computer on a network that requests files from another computer is a:
questions.ex: The client requests services, and the server provides them.

questions.q: Which company developed Windows?
questions.ex: Microsoft developed the Windows operating system.

questions.q: The binary system uses only two digits:
questions.ex: Binary uses 0 (off) and 1 (on).

questions.q: What does 'PDF' stand for?
questions.ex: PDF stands for Portable Document Format.

questions.q: Which device is used for connecting a computer to the internet via phone line?
questions.ex: A modem modulates/demodulates signals for internet access.

questions.q: The intersection of a row and a column in Excel is called a:
questions.ex: A cell is the basic unit of an Excel worksheet.

questions.q: Which of the following is an 'Output' device?
questions.ex: A monitor displays output to the user.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key for 'Copy'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+C is used to copy selected items.

questions.q: A 'Byte' consists of how many bits?
questions.ex: 1 Byte = 8 Bits.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Computers'?
questions.ex: Charles Babbage designed the first mechanical computer.

questions.q: Which software is used for browsing the internet?
questions.ex: Google Chrome is a popular web browser.

questions.q: What does 'GUI' stand for?
questions.ex: GUI allows users to interact with devices through visual icons.

questions.q: How many years did the Holy Quran take for complete revelation?
questions.ex: The Quran was revealed over a period of approximately 23 years.

questions.q: The 'Battle of Badr' took place in which Hijri year?
questions.ex: The Battle of Badr occurred in 2 A.H.

questions.q: Which Prophet is known as 'Abul Bashar'?
questions.ex: Hazrat Adam (AS) is called Abul Bashar (Father of Mankind).

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Zakat'?
questions.ex: Zakat literally means to purify or grow.

questions.q: How many 'Sajdahs' are there in the Holy Quran?
questions.ex: There are 14 mandatory Sajdah-e-Tilawat in the Quran.

questions.q: The first Muazzin of Islam was:
questions.ex: Hazrat Bilal Habshi (RA) was the first Muazzin.

questions.q: Which Surah is called the 'Heart of Quran'?
questions.ex: Surah Yaseen is famously known as the Heart of the Quran.

questions.q: The 'Treaty of Hudaibiya' was signed in:
questions.ex: The treaty was signed in 6 A.H.

questions.q: The largest Surah of the Holy Quran is:
questions.ex: Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah.

questions.q: How many daughters did the Holy Prophet (SAW) have?
questions.ex: The Holy Prophet (SAW) had four daughters: Zainab, Ruqayyah, Umm Kulthum, and Fatima (RA).

questions.q: The 'Objective Resolution' was passed in:
questions.ex: It was passed on March 12, 1949.

questions.q: Which is the highest peak of Pakistan?
questions.ex: K2 (8611m) is the highest in Pakistan and 2nd in the world.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Liaquat Ali Khan was the first PM.

questions.q: The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan was enforced on:
questions.ex: It was enforced on 14 August 1973.

questions.q: The largest province of Pakistan by area is:
questions.ex: Balochistan covers about 44% of Pakistan's land area.

questions.q: Which city is known as the 'City of Lights'?
questions.ex: Karachi is famously called the City of Lights.

questions.q: The 'Lucknow Pact' was signed in:
questions.ex: It was signed in 1916 between Muslim League and Congress.

questions.q: Who wrote the national anthem of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Hafeez Jalandhari wrote the lyrics of the anthem.

questions.q: The 'Tashkent Declaration' was signed in:
questions.ex: It was signed after the 1965 war in January 1966.

questions.q: Which is the national flower of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Jasmine (Chambeli) is the national flower.

questions.q: If 'RED' is coded as 360, what is the code for 'CAT'?
questions.ex: Pattern: Position in alphabet multiplied (C=3, A=1, T=20 -> 3*1*20 = 60).

questions.q: What is 15% of 200?
Find the odd one out: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11
questions.ex: All are prime numbers except 9 (which is divisible by 3).

questions.q: Complete the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ___
questions.ex: These are squares of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Next is 6^2 = 36.

questions.q: A clock shows 3:00. What is the angle between the hands?
questions.ex: At 3:00, the hour hand is at 3 and minute hand at 12, forming 90 degrees.

questions.q: If 10 men can do a work in 5 days, how many days will 5 men take?
questions.ex: Inverse proportion: 10*5 = 5*x -> x = 10.

questions.q: Which number comes next: 100, 90, 80, 70, ___
questions.ex: Subtracting 10 each time.

questions.q: Who has the power to amend the Schedule of the FIA Act?
questions.ex: The Federal Government can amend the Schedule by notification in the official Gazette.

questions.q: The Director General of FIA is appointed by:
questions.ex: Section 3(2) states the DG is appointed by the Federal Government.

questions.q: Under Section 5, an FIA officer has the power of an 'Officer-in-Charge' of a:
questions.ex: Section 5(1) gives powers of an officer-in-charge of a police station.

questions.q: FIA was established to replace which organization?

questions.q: The 'Superintendence' of the Agency vests in the:
questions.ex: Section 4(1) states superintendence vests in the Federal Government.

questions.q: Which section of the FIA Act deals with 'Indemnity' (protection of action taken in good faith)?
questions.ex: Section 8 provides indemnity to the Federal Government and Agency members.

questions.q: The FIA can investigate offenses committed on:
questions.ex: The Act extends to the whole of Pakistan and also applies to citizens/public servants wherever they may be.

questions.q: Which section deals with the 'Power to make rules'?
questions.ex: Section 9 empowers the Federal Government to make rules.

questions.q: The 'Administration' of the Agency vests in the:
questions.ex: Section 4(2) states the administration vests in the Director General.

questions.q: Choose the correct spelling:
questions.ex: The correct spelling is 'Committee' (double m, double t, double e).

questions.ex: 'a' is used before consonant sounds.

questions.ex: We use 'on' for surfaces.

questions.ex: Nouns ending in 'x' take 'es' for plural.

questions.q: Opposite of 'Day':
questions.ex: Night is the opposite of day.

questions.ex: Third-person singular takes 's' in present tense.

questions.ex: Glad means happy.

questions.q: Antonym of 'Cold':
questions.ex: Hot is the opposite of cold.

questions.ex: 'I' takes the base form of the verb.

questions.ex: The correct spelling is 'Grammar'.

questions.ex: A doctor treats patients.

questions.q: The sun '___' in the East.
questions.ex: Universal truths are in simple present tense.

questions.ex: Large and big have the same meaning.

questions.ex: Poor is the opposite of rich.

questions.q: This is '___' umbrella.
questions.ex: 'an' is used before vowel sounds.

questions.ex: 'runs' is an action word (verb).

questions.ex: Chakor is the national bird of Pakistan.

questions.ex: Punjab is the most populous province.

questions.q: How many colors are in the rainbow?
questions.ex: There are 7 colors (VIBGYOR).

questions.ex: The African Elephant is the largest land animal.

questions.q: The smallest continent in the world is:
questions.ex: Australia is the smallest continent.

questions.q: Which city is the capital of Punjab?
questions.ex: Lahore is the provincial capital of Punjab.

questions.q: How many days are in a leap year?
questions.ex: A leap year has 366 days.

questions.q: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.ex: The Pacific Ocean is the largest.

questions.q: The 'Badshahi Mosque' is located in:
questions.ex: It was built by Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in Lahore.

questions.q: Which is the national fruit of Pakistan in summer?
questions.ex: Mango is the national fruit of Pakistan in summer.

questions.q: Who was the first woman Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Benazir Bhutto was the first female PM of any Muslim country.

questions.q: Which is the longest river in Pakistan?
questions.ex: The Indus River is the longest in Pakistan.

questions.q: The national game of Pakistan is:
questions.ex: Field Hockey is the national game.

questions.q: Which country is to the West of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Afghanistan is to the West/Northwest of Pakistan.

questions.q: How many provinces are there in Pakistan?
questions.ex: There are 4 main provinces: Punjab, Sindh, KPK, and Balochistan.

questions.q: The 'Faisal Mosque' is in:
questions.ex: Faisal Mosque is a landmark of Islamabad.

questions.q: Which is the tallest building in the world?
questions.ex: Burj Khalifa in Dubai is the tallest.

questions.q: What is the full form of RAM?
questions.ex: RAM is temporary storage.

questions.q: Which device is used to type text on a computer?
questions.ex: Keyboard is the primary input device for text.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key for 'Paste'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+V is used to paste.

questions.q: Which part of the computer displays the image?
questions.ex: The monitor is the output screen.

questions.q: A computer 'Mouse' is an:
questions.ex: Mouse is used to give commands (input).

questions.q: What is the shortcut key for 'Save'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+S is used to save files.

questions.q: Which of the following is the 'Brain' of computer?
questions.ex: CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the brain.

questions.q: MS Word is used for:
questions.ex: MS Word is a word processor for typing documents.

questions.q: Which of the following is a storage device?
questions.ex: Hard disk stores data permanently.

questions.q: The 'Delete' key is used to:
questions.ex: Delete key erases selected items.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key to 'Print'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+P is the standard print shortcut.

questions.q: Google is a:
questions.ex: Google is the most popular search engine.

questions.q: Which key is used to start a new line in MS Word?
questions.ex: Enter key starts a new paragraph or line.

questions.q: What is the full form of CPU?
questions.ex: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

questions.q: Which device is used to get a hard copy of a document?
questions.ex: Printer provides hard copies on paper.

questions.q: What is the shortcut for 'Select All'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+A selects all content.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a computer hardware?
questions.ex: Windows is an Operating System (Software).

questions.q: 1 Byte is equal to how many bits?
questions.ex: 1 Byte = 8 Bits.

questions.q: Which key is used to capitalize a single letter?
questions.ex: Holding Shift while typing a letter makes it capital.

questions.q: The 'Escape' key is used to:
questions.ex: Esc key cancels or exits the current operation.

questions.q: How many pillars of Islam are there?
questions.ex: There are 5 pillars (Kalma, Namaz, Roza, Zakat, Hajj).

questions.q: Which is the first pillar of Islam?
questions.ex: Faith (Shahadat) is the first pillar.

questions.q: How many times is Namaz obligatory in a day?
questions.ex: Five daily prayers are mandatory.

questions.q: In which month do Muslims fast?
questions.ex: Fasting is mandatory in the month of Ramadan.

questions.q: Where is the Holy Kaaba located?
questions.ex: The Kaaba is in Makkah, Saudi Arabia.

questions.q: Which is the first book of Allah?
questions.ex: Torah was the first revealed book (to Hazrat Musa AS).

questions.q: Who was the first Prophet of Allah?
questions.ex: Hazrat Adam AS was the first human and Prophet.

questions.q: The Holy Quran was revealed to which Prophet?
questions.ex: The Quran was revealed to the last Prophet Muhammad SAW.

questions.q: How many Surahs are in the Holy Quran?
questions.ex: There are 114 Surahs.

questions.q: What is the meaning of 'Islam'?
questions.ex: Islam means peace and submission to Allah.

questions.q: When did Pakistan come into being?
questions.ex: Pakistan gained independence on 14 August 1947.

questions.q: Who gave the idea of a separate state for Muslims?
questions.ex: Allama Iqbal gave the idea in his Allahabad address (1930).

questions.q: What is the national language of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Urdu is the national language.

questions.q: Which is the national flower of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Jasmine is the national flower.

questions.q: The 'Minar-e-Pakistan' is in which city?
questions.ex: It was built where the Pakistan Resolution was passed in Lahore.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Liaquat Ali Khan was the first PM.

questions.q: Which is the national animal of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Markhor is the national animal.

questions.q: How many stanzas are in Pakistan's National Anthem?
questions.ex: The anthem has 3 stanzas.

questions.q: Which is the highest civilian award of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Nishan-e-Pakistan is the highest civilian award.

questions.q: In which year was the first constitution of Pakistan passed?
questions.ex: The first constitution was enforced in 1956.

questions.q: What is 2 + 2 x 2?
questions.ex: According to BODMAS, multiply first: 2 + (2x2) = 2 + 4 = 6.

questions.q: Complete the series: 2, 4, 6, 8, ___
questions.ex: It is a sequence of even numbers.

questions.q: Which number is the largest: 105, 150, 501, 115?
questions.ex: 501 is the largest value.

questions.q: What is the next letter: A, C, E, G, ___
questions.ex: One letter is skipped in between (B, D, F).

questions.q: If a dozen eggs cost 120, what is the cost of 1 egg?

questions.ex: Potato is a vegetable, others are fruits.

questions.q: What is 50% of 100?
questions.ex: Half of 100 is 50.

questions.q: A triangle has how many sides?
questions.ex: A triangle always has 3 sides.

questions.q: If today is Monday, what day was yesterday?
questions.ex: Sunday comes before Monday.

questions.q: What is 100 - 45?
questions.ex: 100 minus 45 equals 55.

questions.q: What does FIA stand for?
questions.ex: FIA is the premier federal investigation agency of Pakistan.

questions.q: The head office of FIA is in:
questions.ex: FIA headquarters is in Islamabad.

questions.q: FIA works under which Ministry?
questions.ex: FIA is under the Ministry of Interior.

questions.q: The main job of a Constable is to:
questions.ex: Constables provide operational and security support.

questions.q: Who is the head of the FIA?
questions.ex: The Agency is headed by a Director General.

questions.q: FIA was established in which year?
questions.ex: FIA was established under the FIA Act 1974.

questions.q: What should a Constable do if they see a crime?
questions.ex: Duty requires reporting and taking lawful action.

questions.q: Is a Constable allowed to take money from public?
questions.ex: Bribery is a crime and strictly prohibited.

questions.q: The uniform of FIA is usually:
questions.ex: Law enforcement uniforms are typically Khaki or Blue variants.

questions.q: Which wing of FIA deals with cyber crimes?
questions.ex: NR3C is the dedicated cyber crime wing of FIA.
